title: apocynum pumilum
intro: Apocynum pumilum, also known as wolf's milk or prickly dogwood, is a perennial herb native to North America. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating various cardiovascular issues, such as high blood pressure and edema, due to its ability to inhibit angiotensin-converting enzyme (ACE). The health benefits of Apocynum pumilum include reducing inflammation, improving circulation, and lowering cholesterol levels. The plant can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or decoctions, and it is often combined with other herbs for enhanced medicinal effects.
